sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 31

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 31:1
items.html: This is a very deep matter. The true reality of the Nukva [female] has no existence except in AK [Adam Kadmon]. And she was hidden away in the RADLA [Reisha de-Lo Ityada, the unknowable head] of Atzilut, since the Malchut of AK clothes in the Rosh [head] of Atik; and from there she will be revealed in the future, in the secret of "the stone the builders rejected has become the cornerstone" — for the cornerstone is the secret of RADLA, which is the first Rosh of Atzilut. And the Nukva of Atzilut that is revealed is the one sweetened in the quality of mercy of the Hey Rishona [first Hey], for Malchut passed there with the Masach [screen] that had been corrected in her, and from there she makes all the Zivugim [couplings]. And you already know that every slight change in the spirituals makes the thing into a matter distinct in itself; hence the sweetened Malchut is considered a different discernment from the Malchut in AK of the first Tzimtzum [restriction]. And there is a great addition in the matter of the sweetened Malchut, for she joined herself to each and every Sefira, in the secret of the Nukva-in-the-body that must be present in every Partzuf, which is the place bearing within it the corrected Masach with the Aviut [coarseness], raising Returning Light and measuring the whole level of the Partzuf. Accordingly, the discernment of the Nukva is present in each and every one of the twelve Partzufim of Atzilut. Moreover, the essence of her existence is in the Partzuf of Keter, for there is Behina Dalet, which is the utmost of her greatness; and from there she goes on diminishing down to her own level, which is of the discernment of the root of Aviut — namely, her own Keter, which is the utmost diminution of the Nukva's existence.

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 31:2
items.html: (to the question) However, the Nukva we are engaged with is not the matter of the Nukva-in-the-body of every Partzuf; the main engagement is with the separated Nukva of ZA [Zeir Anpin], called the Partzuf of Malchut of Atzilut. She was clarified out of the level of Malchut that emerged in Nekudim. And although she is truly the smaller reality of the discernment of Malchut's existence, being only the discernment of her Nefesh, as above — nevertheless she is the last Partzuf of Atzilut for all the holiness of Atzilut, and hence she is the discernment of the intermediary between BYA [Beria, Yetzira, Assiya] of separation and the holy Atzilut. For this reason no correction is imparted to the NRN [Nefesh, Ruach, Neshama] of the righteous, or to any reality in BYA, except through her; hence all the engagement of the wisdom is only with the discernment of this Nukva, for all the governance of the worlds in reward and punishment, until the end of correction, is entirely through her. And the beginning of her emanation comes after the Gadlut of ZA, since the AHP [Awzen, Hotem, Peh] of ZA at the time of Katnut stand with her GE [Galgalta ve Eynaim] in one degree, from the time of the breaking of the vessels. Therefore, when ZA raises his AHP from BYA, he is found to have raised with him also the GE of the Nukva, and she joins with him in the discernment of added MAN [Mayin Nukvin] in AVI [Aba ve Ima], where she receives her first Zivug, still without form. Afterwards she descends with Zeir Anpin, and the Nukva-in-the-body of ZA gives her, through her Zivug with ZA, the discernment of the form of an embryo, and so on, until she receives the completeness of the Mochin of Yenika [suckling]. Then she rises with ZA as MAN a second time to AVI and receives Mochin of VAK [the six ends]; from these Mochin she is sawn apart [Nesira] and becomes a Partzuf distinct from ZA. Thus the beginning of the Nukva's existence has been explained: it begins after the Gadlut of ZA, for then she rises in the discernment of MAN, from BYA to Atzilut. (p. 772, Item 60)
